forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,34 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+depends_on:
+- 01KTCAFH74MPPZ9282P699QBW0
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '8780'
+project: card-comments
+title: Update implement/finish/kanban skills to record a conversation log via add comment
+---
+## What
+Teach the work-the-card skills to record progress as a conversation log on the task using the new `add comment` op. Edit the SKILL.md SOURCE files under `builtin/skills/` (NOT the generated `.skills/` directory — per project convention, `.skills/` is generated).
+
+Files:
+1. `builtin/skills/implement/SKILL.md` — add a short "Record progress" step in the Process section. Guidance: after moving to `doing` and at meaningful milestones (research done, implementation done, blockers hit, before moving to `review`), call `{"op":"add comment","task_id":"<id>","text":"<what happened>"}`. The author is attributed automatically to the dispatching actor. Note the existing rule "Comment what happened, report back" already in the implement skill (step 6 "Cannot complete?") — make that concrete by pointing at `add comment`.
+2. `builtin/skills/finish/SKILL.md` — add equivalent guidance so the autonomous loop records a comment per iteration / state transition.
+3. `builtin/skills/kanban/SKILL.md` — add the same brief guidance for the general "pick up a card" flow.
+
+Keep the additions concise and consistent with each skill's existing voice. Reference the op by its canonical form `add comment` and note `list comments` for reviewing prior context on a card before starting.
+
+Verify there is a build/generation step that regenerates `.skills/` from `builtin/skills/` (do not hand-edit generated output). If the repo has a generation command, run it; otherwise note that generation happens at build time.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+- [ ] implement, finish, and kanban SKILL.md sources each instruct the agent to record a conversation log via `add comment` at defined milestones.
+- [ ] Guidance references the canonical op names `add comment` / `list comments`.
+- [ ] No edits were made directly under `.skills/` (only `builtin/skills/`).
+- [ ] If a skills-generation step exists, generated output reflects the new guidance.
+
+## Tests
+- [ ] Add/extend a skill-content test if the crate has one (search `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills` and `builtin/skills` tests for an existing pattern that asserts skill body content) to assert the implement/finish/kanban skill bodies contain an `add comment` instruction. If no such test harness exists, add a lightweight test in the skills crate that loads the builtin skill markdown and asserts the substring is present.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills` (or the crate that owns builtin skill loading) — green.
+
+## Workflow
+- Use `/tdd` — write the skill-body assertion test first, then edit the SKILL.md sources.
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -9,26 +9,28 @@
 title: Update implement/finish/kanban skills to record a conversation log via add comment
 ---
 ## What
-Teach the work-the-card skills to record progress as a conversation log on the task using the new `add comment` op. Edit the SKILL.md SOURCE files under `builtin/skills/` (NOT the generated `.skills/` directory — per project convention, `.skills/` is generated).
+Teach the work-the-card skills to record progress as a conversation log on the task using the new `add comment` op. Edit the SKILL.md SOURCE files under `builtin/skills/` (NOT any generated copy — per project convention, generated skill dirs are produced from source).
+
+CONFIRMED: sources are `builtin/skills/{implement,finish,kanban}/SKILL.md`. Generation/deploy is handled by `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills` (`deploy.rs`), which produces the generated copies at `.skills/`, `.sah/skills/`, and `apps/kanban-cli/.skills/`. Do NOT hand-edit any of those three — edit only `builtin/skills/` and let deploy regenerate.

 Files:
-1. `builtin/skills/implement/SKILL.md` — add a short "Record progress" step in the Process section. Guidance: after moving to `doing` and at meaningful milestones (research done, implementation done, blockers hit, before moving to `review`), call `{"op":"add comment","task_id":"<id>","text":"<what happened>"}`. The author is attributed automatically to the dispatching actor. Note the existing rule "Comment what happened, report back" already in the implement skill (step 6 "Cannot complete?") — make that concrete by pointing at `add comment`.
-2. `builtin/skills/finish/SKILL.md` — add equivalent guidance so the autonomous loop records a comment per iteration / state transition.
-3. `builtin/skills/kanban/SKILL.md` — add the same brief guidance for the general "pick up a card" flow.
+1. `builtin/skills/implement/SKILL.md` — add a short "Record progress" step in the Process section. Guidance: after moving to `doing` and at meaningful milestones (research done, implementation done, blockers hit, before moving to `review`), call `{"op":"add comment","task_id":"<id>","text":"<what happened>"}`. The author is attributed automatically to the dispatching actor. CONFIRMED there is already a concrete hook to build on: line ~126 "Cannot complete? Do NOT move forward. Comment what happened, report back." — make "Comment what happened" concrete by pointing it at `add comment`.
+2. `builtin/skills/finish/SKILL.md` — equivalent guidance so the autonomous loop records a comment per iteration / state transition.
+3. `builtin/skills/kanban/SKILL.md` — the same brief guidance for the general "pick up a card" flow. Also suggest calling `list comments` first to review prior context on a card before starting.

-Keep the additions concise and consistent with each skill's existing voice. Reference the op by its canonical form `add comment` and note `list comments` for reviewing prior context on a card before starting.
+Keep additions concise and consistent with each skill's voice. Reference ops by canonical form `add comment` / `list comments`.

-Verify there is a build/generation step that regenerates `.skills/` from `builtin/skills/` (do not hand-edit generated output). If the repo has a generation command, run it; otherwise note that generation happens at build time.
+After editing sources, run the skills deploy/generation (if a command exists, e.g. via the CLI or a cargo xtask) so the generated copies reflect the change; otherwise note generation happens at build/deploy time and leave generated dirs untouched.

 ## Acceptance Criteria
 - [ ] implement, finish, and kanban SKILL.md sources each instruct the agent to record a conversation log via `add comment` at defined milestones.
 - [ ] Guidance references the canonical op names `add comment` / `list comments`.
-- [ ] No edits were made directly under `.skills/` (only `builtin/skills/`).
-- [ ] If a skills-generation step exists, generated output reflects the new guidance.
+- [ ] No edits were made directly under any generated dir (`.skills/`, `.sah/skills/`, `apps/kanban-cli/.skills/`) — only `builtin/skills/`.
+- [ ] If a skills-generation/deploy step is run, generated output reflects the new guidance.

 ## Tests
-- [ ] Add/extend a skill-content test if the crate has one (search `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills` and `builtin/skills` tests for an existing pattern that asserts skill body content) to assert the implement/finish/kanban skill bodies contain an `add comment` instruction. If no such test harness exists, add a lightweight test in the skills crate that loads the builtin skill markdown and asserts the substring is present.
-- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills` (or the crate that owns builtin skill loading) — green.
+- [ ] Add/extend a skill-content test in `crates/swissarmyhammer-skills` (search its tests for an existing pattern that loads a builtin skill body via the skill loader) asserting the implement/finish/kanban skill bodies contain an `add comment` instruction. If no harness exists, add a lightweight test that loads the builtin skill markdown through the crate's loader and asserts the substring.
+- [ ] `cargo nextest run -p swissarmyhammer-skills` — green.

 ## Workflow
 - Use `/tdd` — write the skill-body assertion test first, then edit the SKILL.md sources.
\ No newline at end of file
